title: Create Responsive Navbar Component
description: Develop a new Navbar component that is responsive, context-aware, and styled with Tailwind CSS.
details:
- Create `apps/app/components/Navbar.tsx` as a functional React component using TypeScript.
- Use semantic HTML elements (`<nav>`, `<ul>`, `<li>`, `<button>`) and ARIA attributes for accessibility.
- Implement responsive design using Tailwind CSS utility classes, including a hamburger menu for mobile/tablet viewports.
- Manage the open/closed state of the mobile menu with local component state.
- Define navigation links in a configuration array for maintainability.
- Use the routing library's `Link` component for navigation, ensuring active link styling is applied.
- Ensure the component is visually consistent with the existing design system.
test_strategy:
Write unit tests (e.g., with React Testing Library) to verify:
- Navbar renders correctly on desktop and mobile viewports.
- Hamburger menu toggles visibility of links on mobile.
- Active link styling is applied based on current route.
- Accessibility: correct roles, labels, and keyboard navigation.

title: Integrate Authentication State into Navbar
description: Connect the Navbar component to the authentication state to conditionally render navigation links.
details:
- Import authentication state from `apps/app/lib/store.ts` or use a dedicated auth hook from `apps/app/lib/auth.ts`.
- Conditionally render navigation links: show 'Profile' and 'Logout' for authenticated users; 'Login' and 'Sign Up' for guests.
- Ensure logout action is handled correctly (e.g., by calling an auth method or dispatching an action).
- Keep the Navbar component focused on presentation; delegate logic to hooks or context where possible.
test_strategy:
Write tests to verify:
- Correct set of links is shown for authenticated vs. unauthenticated users.
- Logout button triggers the appropriate action and updates the UI.
- Navbar updates in response to authentication state changes.

title: Replace Existing Navigation with New Navbar
description: Integrate the new Navbar component into the main layout or root route component, replacing any existing navigation bar.
details:
- Locate the current navigation implementation in `apps/app/components/layout.tsx` or `apps/app/routes/__root.tsx`.
- Remove the old navigation markup.
- Import and render the new `<Navbar />` component in the appropriate place to ensure it appears on all pages.
- Ensure the layout remains consistent and the Navbar does not interfere with other persistent UI elements.
test_strategy: Manually verify that the new Navbar appears across all routes and the old navigation is fully removed. Run smoke tests to ensure no layout regressions.

title: Test and Validate Navbar Functionality
description: Perform comprehensive testing of the Navbar component, including responsiveness, authentication state, and accessibility.
details:
- Write component and integration tests (using React Testing Library and Jest) for the Navbar.
- Test rendering for both authenticated and unauthenticated states.
- Test responsive behavior (desktop, tablet, mobile) and hamburger menu interactions.
- Validate accessibility (keyboard navigation, ARIA attributes, screen reader support).
- Perform manual exploratory testing in different browsers and devices.
test_strategy:
Run automated test suite and perform manual QA:
- All tests pass for Navbar scenarios.
- No accessibility violations (use tools like axe-core).
- Navbar behaves correctly on all supported devices and browsers.
